evals.prompt: Creá un post de Instagram para anunciar el lanzamiento de nuestra app. El título es 'Ya disponible en App Store y Google Play'.
evals.expected_output: Should identify this needs text ('Ya disponible en App Store y Google Play') so Canva is the right tool, not generate_image. Should use designType 'social_media' for Instagram. Should call canva_create with a descriptive title. Should return the editUrl so the user can customize the design. Should ask for brand colors if not provided, or proceed with a generic design.
assertions: Chooses canva_create over generate_image because design requires readable text
Uses designType 'social_media'
Calls canva_create with title and designType
Returns editUrl for user to customize
Asks or acknowledges brand color/style information

evals.prompt: Necesito exportar el banner de campaña que tenemos en Canva a PDF para mandarlo a imprenta. El designId es 'DAFxxx123'.
evals.expected_output: Should call canva_export with the provided designId and format 'pdf'. Should note that PDF is the correct format for print. Should wait for the export to complete and return the download URL.
assertions: Calls canva_export with designId 'DAFxxx123'
Uses 'pdf' format (correct for print)
Returns the export download URL
Does not create a new design — uses the existing designId

evals.prompt: Hacé un flyer para un evento de networking el próximo jueves. Tema: IA y Startups. Lugar: WeWork Palermo. Hora: 18:30hs.
evals.expected_output: Should use designType 'poster' for a flyer/event. Should call canva_create with a descriptive title like 'Flyer Networking IA y Startups'. Should return editUrl for the user to add the specific event details and customize. Should note the information to include: event title, date/time, location.
assertions: Uses designType 'poster' for event flyer
Calls canva_create with appropriate title
Returns editUrl
Notes or structures the event details to include in the design

evals.prompt: Quiero una imagen artística para el fondo del banner de LinkedIn. Sin texto, solo algo visual que represente innovación y tecnología.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize that 'no text, purely visual' means generate_image is the right tool here, not Canva. Should use 4:3 aspect ratio for LinkedIn banner. Should construct a detailed artistic prompt around innovation/technology themes. Should call generate_image. May optionally suggest that if the user later wants to add text/logo, they can use Canva with this image as background.
assertions: Correctly routes to generate_image (not Canva) for text-free visual
Uses 4:3 aspect ratio for LinkedIn
Constructs detailed prompt around innovation/technology themes
Optionally suggests combining with Canva for text overlay

evals.prompt: Necesito 5 posts de Instagram con distintos diseños para la semana. Todos sobre consejos de productividad.
evals.expected_output: Should create 5 Canva designs using canva_create, each with a distinct title (e.g., 'Tip 1 - Productividad', 'Tip 2 - Productividad'). Should use designType 'social_media' for all. Should call canva_create 5 times or explain it will create them sequentially. Should return all 5 editUrls so the user can customize each one with the specific tip content.
assertions: Creates 5 separate Canva designs
Uses designType 'social_media' for all
Gives each design a distinct descriptive title
Returns all editUrls
Does not create just one and ask — proceeds to create all 5
